response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack software engineer with strong experience in web, mobile, and distributed backend systems, but the role demands deep specialization in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows no experience in any of these core areas, no deep-learning workload optimization, and no chip/architecture background. The educational path (Bachelor's in CS) plus experience could meet the years threshold, but the domain mismatch is fundamental.
strengths: Extensive software engineering experience across many stacks and domains
Some low-latency, high-throughput systems work (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby) and C/C++ at National Instruments
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, and embedded systems exposure
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator, or GPU architecture experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or hardware-software co-design background
Career is software/web-centric, not performance/hardware architecture at principal level
